# ADR-260002: Archive content curation strategy for the public site

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-05-21
Owners: Repository owner

## Context

The `crystord_platform_archive` repository contains the previous Angular-based public-facing site.
It includes a landing page, Google Add-on marketing content, Add-on legal/support pages, and some
auth-oriented actions. The new site is an Astro static site hosted on GitHub Pages and is not a
technology migration of the Angular app. The archive must therefore be treated as a content and
feature reference, not as a source of implementation patterns.

## Decision

- The new site will be implemented using the approved Astro-based stack, establishing its own modern routing, component model, and design system.
- Preserve these public-content capabilities in the new site:
  - Crystord product overview on the landing page.
  - Google Add-on overview as a dedicated page.
  - Add-on-specific Privacy, Terms, and Support pages.
  - Contact path and YouTube reference.
  - Google trademark notice where Add-on content references Google products.
- Rework these items instead of porting them verbatim:
  - Landing page hero copy and product messaging.
  - Feature summaries and positioning copy.
  - Footer/legal navigation and contact call-to-action.
  - Support instructions and legal text, after review for accuracy.
- Drop these items unless later product validation justifies them:
  - Newsletter promotion.
  - Landing-page sign-up flow.
  - Demo login flow.
- Exclude app-internal routes and authenticated surfaces from the public-site migration.

## Alternatives Considered

- Port the archive pages as-is: Rejected because it would carry forward Angular-specific design,
  generic marketing copy, and auth portal behavior that do not fit the new static-site goals.
- Rebuild everything from scratch without archive reference: Rejected because the archive contains
  valid legal, support, and add-on content that should be reviewed and selectively preserved.
- Keep all visible archive landing-page elements: Rejected because some elements are optional,
  stale, or depend on product capabilities that may not exist in the new site.

## Consequences

- Positive: The new site stays focused on current product goals instead of inheriting old UI and
  implementation constraints.
- Positive: Legal/compliance content remains traceable and can be reviewed before publication.
- Negative: Some archive copy must be manually reviewed and rewritten rather than reused quickly.
- Risk: If legal/support content is copied without validation, the new public site may publish
  outdated or inaccurate information.

## Verification

- The new site contains a landing page, dedicated Google Add-on page, and Add-on legal/support
  pages.
- Public-site navigation contains the curated items only.
- The new implementation will use the approved Astro-based stack and modern component patterns.
- Dropped items are omitted unless a later backlog item explicitly reintroduces them.

## Rollback

If the curated scope is too narrow, create a new backlog item to reintroduce a dropped capability
with explicit rationale. If preserved content proves inaccurate, remove the affected page or section
until corrected.

- Related requirement(s): REQ-FR-260002.md
- Related backlog item(s): BI-260002.md
